result:
# Freeform

Let me talk about listening today, the way a room becomes different when someone finally hears the part you thought you had to hide. I noticed this in a small grocery checkout line last Tuesday, when the cashier asked how my day was and I answered without scrolling my phone. A bag of apples sat in a small brown bag. The fluorescent lights flickered just enough to remind me of hospitals and classrooms, and we both laughed at a joke I did not tell well. Listening slowed time without making it heavy, like when your breath settles and your shoulders fall and the part of you that has been braced finally unclenches. I walked out and the air had more texture. People sometimes mistake listening for agreement, but it is mostly agreement with the truth that another person exists right here with you. Sometimes truth is a stray dog that follows you into the story and eats your sandwich; sometimes it is a candle in a window that means there is a kitchen somewhere nearby. You can listen to weather or the way someone tells a story about their mother’s recipes. You can listen to the inside of your own doubts until they reveal the shape of the door they want. A small map appears: stop, breathe, notice one thing you can see, hear, taste; name it softly; ask someone how they are; give three true details about your day; end by thanking someone or a tree. Attention is a practice of kindness, and kindness is the slow road back to courage. Thank you for letting me practice.
